text.title: Common App Router antipatterns and agent gotchas
text.summary: Recurring mistakes when generating App Router code: misplaced use client, synchronous params in Next.js 15, and middleware importing Node.js-only modules.

antipattern.description: Adding 'use client' to a page or layout that only needs interactivity in one child component.
antipattern.reason: Converts the entire route segment to client-rendered code, breaking SSR, streaming, and direct DB access for the whole page.

rule.statement: Move 'use client' to the smallest child component that actually uses useState, useEffect, event handlers, or browser APIs.
rule.rationale: Keeps parent pages as Server Components so they can stream, fetch directly from the DB, and ship zero client JS for their own markup.

antipattern.description: Accessing params.id synchronously in a dynamic page component trained on Next.js 13/14 patterns.
antipattern.reason: Next.js 15 changed params and searchParams to be Promises. Synchronous access returns undefined at runtime without a visible compile error.

rule.statement: Always type params as Promise and await it before accessing fields in Next.js 15+ projects.
rule.rationale: The async change was introduced to support streaming and partial prerendering; synchronous access is a deprecated behavior.

section.title: Server Action payload limit for file uploads

antipattern.description: Sending file uploads through a Server Action form.
antipattern.reason: Server Actions have a 1 MB default payload limit. Files larger than this will silently fail or error at runtime.

rule.statement: Use presigned URL upload (e.g., Uploadthing, S3 presigned POST) for file uploads. The Server Action only receives the resulting file URL, not the binary data.
rule.rationale: Presigned URLs bypass the Server Action payload limit by uploading directly from the browser to storage.

antipattern.description: Importing Node.js-only modules (e.g., fs, path, crypto) in middleware.ts.
antipattern.reason: Middleware runs on the Edge runtime which has a limited Node.js API surface. Importing unavailable modules causes silent build failures.

rule.statement: Middleware must only use Edge-compatible APIs. Use next-auth/jwt getToken (Edge-compatible) for auth checks; do not import full Node.js auth libraries.
rule.rationale: Edge runtime is a subset of Node.js APIs. The build may succeed locally with experimental flags but fail in production deployment.

antipattern.description: Using matcher: ['/'] or matcher: ['/:path*'] in middleware config, intercepting static assets and API routes.
antipattern.reason: Running middleware on every request including _next/static assets and public files adds latency and can break static asset delivery.

rule.statement: Scope middleware matchers to specific path prefixes (e.g., '/dashboard/:path*', '/api/protected/:path*'). Explicitly exclude static assets if using a broad pattern.
rule.rationale: Middleware runs at the edge on every matched request; broad matchers multiply unnecessary latency.

text.reference: Prompt for scaffolding a complete App Router page
